extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters against and filed by the parties. I counted only the against rows: criminal proceedings against the Company (2), Subsidiaries (1), and Directors (3), excluding all filed-by rows; regulatory/statutory actions against the Company (30), Corporate Promoter (5), and Directors (2), while keeping tax separate; and tax proceedings against the Company (8 indirect + 8 direct) and Subsidiaries (5 indirect + 6 direct). The quantified aggregate against the Company and Subsidiaries is ₹20.94 million for the Company (₹1.21 million regulatory/statutory + ₹19.45 million indirect tax + ₹0.28 million direct tax) plus ₹220.27 million for Subsidiaries (₹35.09 million indirect tax + ₹185.18 million direct tax), totaling ₹241.21 million, converted to ₹24.121 crore. The contingent-liabilities table is dated March 31, 2021 and states a Total of ₹239.27 million for the tax-authority claims subsection; this stated Total is used without re-summing its components and converts to ₹23.927 crore. The disclosed concentration percentages are segment-level only: KFC and Pizza Hut revenue represented 76.08%, 77.49% and 92.28% of total revenue from operations in Fiscals 2019, 2020 and 2021, respectively, and Core Brands delivery/in-store dining percentages are also segment-level; therefore no company-wide top-five or top-ten customer percentage is extracted. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10.
